description: Top-level program encompassing all elements of the NASA-led international Lunar Gateway space station in Near-Rectilinear Halo Orbit (NRHO). Total lifecycle baseline FY2020-FY2033.
Program-level management functions including control, reporting, contracts, safety, and international coordination for the Gateway Program Office (GPO) at JSC.
Earned value management (EVM), program-level cost and schedule reporting, integrated baseline reviews (IBR), and program performance metrics.
Contracting officer technical representatives (COTRs), contract administration, procurement strategy, source selections, and bilateral modification management.
Independent safety reviews, hazard analysis, reliability and quality assurance oversight, human rating compliance (NPR 8705.2C), and nuclear safety (if applicable).
Formal coordination with ESA, JAXA, CSA, and MBRSC via Joint Coordination Boards, Implementing Arrangements, and semiannual partner reviews.
Program-level Configuration Control Boards (CCBs), baseline documentation, engineering change proposal (ECP) process management, and product baseline control.
Integrated Master Schedule (IMS) maintenance in Primavera P6, critical path analysis, schedule risk assessments, and monthly EVM data analysis.
Congressional reporting, GAO and IG response coordination, public affairs, and outreach to external stakeholders and international diplomacy support.
End-to-end systems engineering covering requirements, interfaces, mass, analysis, risk, human factors, technology maturation, and verification and validation planning.
Gateway system-level and derived requirements development, allocation, verification planning, and maintenance of the Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM).
Interface Control Documents (ICDs) between all Gateway elements, visiting vehicles, and partner modules; includes mechanical, electrical, thermal, and data interfaces.
Mass margin tracking, current best estimate (CBE) reporting, center-of-gravity analysis, and mass reduction coordination; primary risk per GAO-24-106878.
Trajectory design, NRHO maintenance analysis, delta-V budgeting, visiting vehicle accommodation, mission design, and orbital mechanics assessments.
Program risk register maintenance, probabilistic risk assessment (PRA), failure modes and effects analysis (FMEA), and reliability predictions.

element_name: Human Factors and Crew Systems Engineering
description: Human-machine interface standards, habitability assessments, crew workload analysis, and human rating compliance per NASA-STD-3001.
Technology Readiness Level (TRL) tracking, critical technology gap closure plans, and technology infusion planning for key Gateway systems.
V&V plan development, test requirement allocation, and verification method selection (test, analysis, inspection, demonstration) for all Gateway requirements.
Solar electric propulsion spacecraft providing primary power (~60 kW BOL), thrust, attitude control, and communications relay. Maxar Technologies FFP contract ($375M).
Primary load-bearing bus structure, deployment mechanisms, separation systems, and structural interface hardware with HALO.
Advanced Roll-Out Solar Array (ROSA) wings providing ~60 kW beginning-of-life power; heritage from ISS ROSA flight demonstration.
Advanced Electric Propulsion System (AEPS) Hall-effect ion thrusters (4 x 12.5 kW), xenon propellant tanks, feed system, and power processing units.
Attitude determination and control system (ADCS), star trackers, inertial measurement units, and reaction control system for fine attitude management.
High-gain antenna for Earth communication, proximity link transponders for visiting vehicles, and deep space relay capability interface.
Radiator panels, heat pipes, thermoelectric heaters, and multi-layer insulation (MLI) blankets for the cis-lunar thermal environment.
Flight computers, data buses, mass memory, avionics architecture, and data management for autonomous operations during crewed-off periods.
On-board software for all PPE subsystems including autonomous fault management and 12-month solo demonstration operations software.
First pressurized crew module providing short-duration habitation, docking ports, logistics storage, and life support. Northrop Grumman prime ($1.122B total).

element_name: HALO Primary Structure and Pressure Vessel
description: Aluminum pressure vessel and primary structural frames; fabricated by Thales Alenia Space Turin, Italy; heritage from Cygnus-derived module design.

element_name: HALO Environmental Control and Life Support
description: Atmosphere revitalization, water recovery, thermal and humidity control, fire detection and suppression for up to 4 crew for short-duration visits.
Power distribution unit (PDU), secondary power management, battery storage, and crew equipment power outlets; interfaces with PPE primary power.
Intra-vehicle audio/video, inter-module communication with Gateway elements, and UHF/S-band/Ka-band proximity and relay links.
Internal thermal fluid loops, external radiator panels, cold plates for avionics cooling, and fluid loop interface with PPE thermal system.
Integrated avionics unit, fault detection isolation and recovery (FDIR) software, and operational software for crewed and extended uncrewed modes.
Five International Docking Standard (IDS)-compatible ports for Orion, HLS, I-HAB, ESPRIT, and Lunar Airlock; active and passive docking capability.

element_name: HALO Interior Outfitting and Crew Systems
description: Crew quarters, galley, waste management, stowage racks, lighting, crew medical equipment, and EVA suit interface provisions.
Polyethylene storm shelter, water-wall radiation mitigation, and supplemental shielding for solar particle event (SPE) crew protection in cis-lunar environment.
ESA-led habitation module with JAXA ECLSS contribution; significantly expands crew habitation volume and life support for longer Artemis lunar missions.
Pressure vessel, primary structure, and external interfaces; sized for co-manifested launch on SLS Block 1B as secondary payload with Orion.
Environmental Control and Life Support System designed and delivered by JAXA; includes water recovery and air revitalization for expanded crew capacity.
Internal fluid loops, external panel radiators, and interface connectors to Gateway integrated thermal management system.
Intra-module and inter-module communication systems, avionics unit, and software integration with Gateway core avionics architecture.
Standardized payload racks, power and data interfaces for scientific instruments and ESA technology demonstrations in lunar vicinity.

element_name: I-HAB Interior Outfitting and Crew Systems
description: Additional crew quarters, recreation area, stowage, viewports, and crew interface panels expanding habitation beyond HALO baseline.
Co-manifested SLS payload adapter, structural separation system, and integration with Artemis IV launch campaign at KSC.
Polyethylene-based supplemental radiation shielding panels and SPE shelter provisions for the expanded habitation volume in NRHO.
ESA contribution consisting of Lunar Link (launched 2027 with HALO) and Lunar View (2029) providing communications relay and xenon propellant refueling.
External Ka-band high-rate communication system pre-attached to HALO before launch; provides lunar surface relay and deep space data rates.
Pressurized module with viewports, xenon propellant storage, additional docking port, and habitation corridor; designed under Oct 2024 ESA amendment.
High-pressure xenon storage tanks, on-orbit transfer lines, valves, and interface hardware for PPE Solar Electric Propulsion system resupply.
Laser communications terminal providing multi-Gbps data link capability; technology demonstration and operational supplemental link.

element_name: ESPRIT Docking Ports and Interface Hardware
description: Passive IDS-compatible docking ports, structural interfaces, and pass-through electrical and fluid connections for Gateway integration.
ESA ground segment elements supporting ESPRIT operations from ESOC Darmstadt; includes data relay scheduling and refueling operations monitoring.
Airlock module contributed by Mohammed Bin Rashid Space Centre (UAE) enabling extravehicular activity (EVA) directly from Gateway; announced January 2024.

element_name: Airlock Primary Structure and Pressure Vessel
description: Double-hatch airlock vessel capable of full depressurization and repressurization cycles supporting Gateway EVA operations in the lunar vicinity.
Spacesuit donning/doffing aids, tether attachment points, EVA tools storage, and suit interface panels for lunar spacesuit operations.
IDS-compatible structural and fluid interface adapters for connection to Gateway HALO or I-HAB docking port.
Pressure control avionics, hatch actuators, depressurization monitoring instrumentation, and safety interlock systems.
Emergency pressure relief valves, crew rescue provisions, fire suppression system, and abort repressurization capability.
EVA suit maintenance tools, EVA consumables stowage, crew restraint systems, and equipment transfer provisions between airlock and cabin.
Thermal control for airlock structure and crew equipment including heaters, MLI blankets, and interface to Gateway thermal management system.
Canadian Space Agency robotic system including 17m large arm, small dexterous arm, and AI autonomy software for external maintenance and element assembly.
17-meter large robotic arm for element assembly, module berthing, and large payload handling; end-effector compatible with all Gateway base points.
Advanced small arm for fine manipulation, inspection, and servicing tasks; AI-enabled autonomous operations capability.

element_name: External Robotic Interfaces and Base Points
description: Latching end effectors, power/data/video connectors on all Gateway module hulls, and robotic interface compatibility hardware.
Canadian ground control segment for Canadarm3 telerobotics, operator training simulators, and mission planning tools at CSA Saint-Hubert.
Autonomous robotic operations software, AI-enabled inspection algorithms, anomaly detection, and incremental fault recovery autonomy.
End-to-end integration of large arm, small arm, and robotic interfaces; acceptance testing, flight certification, and delivery for Gateway installation.
End-to-end integration and environmental testing of Gateway elements at NGIS Gilbert AZ and KSC; includes GSE, hazardous operations, and contamination control.
Physical mating of PPE (Maxar-built) and HALO modules at Northrop Grumman Innovation Systems (NGIS) facility in Gilbert, Arizona.
Acoustic, vibration, thermal vacuum, electromagnetic compatibility (EMC), and structural qualification testing of PPE-HALO integrated stack.
Xenon loading, hydrazine RCS fueling, high-pressure pressurant servicing, and hypergolic propellant handling safety operations.
End-to-end software and hardware interface testing, avionics-to-avionics compatibility verification, and integrated operational scenario simulations.
Transportation to Kennedy Space Center PHSF, mate to Falcon Heavy fairing, final checkouts, encapsulation, and countdown preparations.
Test stands, handling fixtures, electrical GSE, purge systems, and clean room facilities at NGIS Gilbert AZ and KSC.
Cleanliness verification, particulate and molecular contamination monitoring, purge gas systems, and workmanship standards throughout IA&T.
KSC Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility (PHSF) operations, propellant loading procedures, range safety compliance, and crew emergency procedures.
Launch vehicle procurement and mission integration services for all Gateway elements across multiple launches 2027-2031.
SpaceX Falcon Heavy Block 5 Expendable launch of PPE-HALO stack; $331.8M firm-fixed-price contract; NET April 2027 from KSC LC-39A.
Co-manifested I-HAB on SLS Block 1B with Orion and Artemis IV crew; SLS cost captured in SLS/Orion program; Gateway incurs integration costs only.
Commercial launch of ESPRIT Lunar View module; NET July 2029; launch vehicle contract under development; estimate based on Falcon 9 Heavy equivalent.

element_name: UAE Airlock Launch (Artemis VI or Commercial)
description: Launch of UAE Airlock module; planned as SLS Artemis VI co-manifested payload or dedicated commercial vehicle; NET October 2030.

element_name: KSC Payload Processing and Launch Site Operations
description: KSC payload processing contractor support, payload processing facility use agreements, hazardous servicing support, and launch site logistics.
Range safety documentation, flight termination system certification, range scheduling coordination, and launch weather assessment services.
Ground-based infrastructure including mission control, Deep Space Network integration, flight dynamics, data management, and partner interfaces.
Dedicated Gateway flight control room at MCC-H Johnson Space Center; workstation development, staffing plan, display systems, and facility modifications.
DSN antenna scheduling agreements, Ka/X-band link budget certification, contact time allocation, and operational interagency agreements.
NRHO trajectory determination, stationkeeping maneuver planning, orbit maintenance operations, and conjunction assessment services.
Telemetry processing, data archiving and distribution, science data downlink pipeline, and onboard software upload capability.
Network connections, data exchange agreements, and interface specifications for ESA ESOC, JAXA TKSC, CSA Saint-Hubert, and MBRSC control centers.
Cybersecurity architecture, accreditation (ATO) under FISMA, intrusion detection, and information assurance for Gateway mission systems.
Personnel, training, procedures, mission planning, science coordination, crew health, and visiting vehicle operations for Gateway crewed and uncrewed phases.
Flight directors, flight controllers, mission operations directorate (MOD) staffing, shift scheduling, and certification program for crewed Gateway missions.
Gateway systems training for Artemis crews, EVA training, emergency procedures development, and flight procedures verification and validation.
Detailed mission timeline development, activity constraint management, resource allocation, and real-time mission execution planning.
Science payload scheduling, data return planning, payload operator training, and science team coordination for Gateway science program.
Multi-center real-time coordination with ESA ESOC, JAXA TKSC, CSA, and MBRSC for joint crewed mission operations and planning.
Crew health monitoring, medical countermeasures for cis-lunar radiation, telemedicine protocols, and flight surgeon support for Gateway missions.
Approach corridor management, docking/undocking coordination, visiting vehicle monitoring, and Orion and HLS integrated timeline support.
Ground-in-the-loop operations during between-mission uncrewed periods; anomaly response, stationkeeping coordination, and system health monitoring.
